name: Divine Delicacies - Storytelling and Menu Tasting Session
description:
Divine Delicacies offers a sensory journey through India's sacred kitchens. Join storyteller Aparna Jaishankar and Bharatnatyam dancer and educator Rajeshwari Aravind to uncover the myths, history, and folklore behind beloved temple prasadams through a very unique source- their architecture! 
As Aparna shares the deep-rooted myths, legends, and folklores of ancient temples, the audience will taste authentic prasadams uniquely tied to each temple’s deity. Rajeshwari will unpack the silent language of these divine spaces by showcasing images of the frozen narratives of gopurams, pillars, and carvings, the sacred geometry and designs that turned stones into a spiritual canvas, and decode a few of the cultural motifs in these visual stories.
Experience Temple architecture brought to life through food and stories.
About Rajeshwari:
Bharatanatyam has shaped Rajeshwari Aravind’s world for as long as she can remember. What began as a childhood initiation grew into an enduring pursuit marked by constant exploration and refinement. Rajeshwari works for Aalaap, an Arts Consulting and Management company based in Chennai. She is also involved in organising, archiving and documenting a wide range of artistic projects in the field of dance.

name: Magnet and Keychain Making Workshop - Art Workshop
description:
Join me for a creative workshop of magnet and keychain making using acrylic paints.
This is a beginner-friendly session where you can explore your artistic talent and express them in the form of unique and vibrant keychains and magnets that capture your vivid imagination. Personalised art pieces to take home that would be an expression of your inner self!
What’s provided
All the materials will be provided, along with expert guidance
What you need to bring
Just bring your creative vibes along

name: TIME — Official Premiere - Film Screening
description:
Film Logline:
In a world where time is treated as currency, four people trapped inside a system that measures human existence are forced to confront what a life is worth—and what love refuses to surrender.
About the screening: 
What if time were not simply something we lived through, but something that could be measured, priced, issued and taken away?
TIME is an English-language psychological science-fiction short film from BackstoryAlley Productions. Set within a stark black-box world, the film follows four lives caught inside a system that measures human existence with ruthless precision.
As a mother and daughter confront the cost of a life reduced to minutes, transactions, and receipts, the boundaries between participant, observer, and storyteller begin to fracture. Intimate, unsettling and deeply human, TIME explores memory, grief, sacrifice and the quiet violence of believing that everything valuable can be counted.
The screening at Atta Galatta marks the film’s first official premiere. It will be followed by a brief interaction with the filmmaker and members of the production team.
CAST: Wayne Moohan, Lisa Alexandra, Gráinne Kelly, Lynn Traboulsy
KEY CREDITS
A film by: Aashrith G R
Producers:
Aashrith G R, N Gangadhar Raju, Shreyas Srihari, Mridhula Sriharsha
Executive Producers:
Himani Bajaj, Mugunthan Balaji
Director of Photography: Daksh Hemdev
Associate Production Company:, 8Lens Production
Studio Partner: Sunset Studios, London
Production Company: BackstoryAlley Productions

name: Meet the Author: Indrani Talukdar - Book Discussion
description:
What happens when love becomes a matter of life and death?
Join award-winning author Indrani Talukdar in conversation with Reena Pereira as they discuss Till the Dust Settles, a compelling collection of fiction that moves between danger, humour, and the complexities of everyday life in India.
At the heart of the book is a gripping novelette about a couple forced to flee their rural community after an illicit romance puts them in mortal danger. Alongside it are witty and insightful stories of small-town India—featuring eccentric dowry negotiations, family dramas, unexpected acts of courage, and moments of quiet transformation.
Drawing from her experiences across India and abroad, Talukdar's writing explores the tensions between tradition and change, individual desire and social expectation, while capturing the humour, contradictions, and resilience of ordinary lives.
Whether you're a lover of fiction, curious about contemporary Indian storytelling, or simply looking for an engaging literary evening, this is an opportunity to meet the author, hear about her writing journey, and participate in a lively discussion followed by audience Q&A.
Author: Indrani Talukdar
Moderator: Reena Pereira
Date: Sunday, 19 July 2026
Time: 5:30 PM – 6:30 PM
Venue: Atta Galatta, 178, 5th Main, 9th Cross, First Stage, Indiranagar, Bengaluru

name: Rhyme & Rasam - Poetry That’s Easy to Love
description:
Come, enjoy an evening of poetry about rivers and all the emotions they evoke. This is a guided reading with carefully-chosen poems. We won't torture the poems through over-analysis. We will examine them lightly and read them in context.
You don't even have to love poetry to join. Come for some literary comfort and some surprises. Depth of flavour assured!
Theme: Poems about Rivers
Hosted by writer and poet Suchi Govindarajan
What's included:
Tea and Coffee

name: Paper Money of the Princely State of Hyderabad - Book launch
description:
Widely recognized as the Co-Founder of Prestige Estates Projects Ltd. and the Managing Director of Prestige Group, Rezwan is equally revered as a pioneering numismatist and a passionate preservationist of Indian Paper Money.
This launch celebrates Rezwan’s ongoing dedication to uncovering the rich history of India's currency. This work explores the unique Osmania Sicca currency system under the Nizam’s rule.
As Rezwan works on future publications, including a volume on the Banknotes of French India and Portuguese India, this book launch is a testament to his belief that banknotes reflect the historical evolution of a nation and serve as a common factor for all individuals. We are thrilled to celebrate his expanding legacy right here in his home city.

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Borders are more than lines on a map; they are stories of power, ambition, and the lives they reshape. This month, we journey through the forgotten history of The Great Hedge of India, uncovering an extraordinary colonial experiment that sought to control trade and people through an immense living barrier. What does it mean to rediscover the hidden landscapes of history and the legacies they leave behind?
 
Book of the month: The Great Hedge of India by Roy Moxham
When: Saturday, 25th July, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
 
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots.
 
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a community for people who love to read and eat.

name: How to become a Web-Comic Creator - Comic Making Workshop
description:
Want to create comics or illustrations and don’t know where to start? Not a pro at sketching, but have great visual ideas that you want to put out on Instagram? This workshop is for you.
Corporat Comics, inspired by the proverbial corporate rat race, came to life when Shubham Khurana, a marketer by day and an undercover artist by night, decided to combine his passion for sketching with the humdrum and inaneness of corporate life. After Monday to Friday and Work-Life Imbalance, the ‘rat’ from Corporat Comics is now back with his third collection of comics titled Humans of Corporate.
Join us on Saturday, July 25, 11 am-2 pm IST at Atta Galatta for a four-part workshop with Shubham on what it takes to feel inspired, sketch out your brand, pin your medium, and promote your work to become a web comic creator finally.
This workshop will cover:
· How to get started
· What tools to use
· Finding inspiration
· Creating a bank of ideas
· Converting ideas to visuals
· Storyboarding
· Sketching it out
· Inking and colouring
· Lettering
· Building your own style
Please Note: You do NOT need to be good at sketching to make the most of this workshop :)
While some basic stationery will be provided by the bookstore, guests are free to experiment with any physical or digital medium of their choice.
 
Instagram: @simonandschusterin
X: @SimonSchusterIN
Facebook: @simonandschusterindia
LinkedIn: Simon & Schuster India

name: AG Lit Sisters Presents: Around the World -
description:
If you could recommend just one South-East Asian book to everyone, which one would it be and why? 
Our first destination is South-East Asia, home to stories that are as diverse as its cultures. From intimate family sagas and coming-of-age tales to historical fiction, folklore, and contemporary voices, we'll explore books that transport us across Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Vietnam, Thailand, the Philippines, and beyond.
Whether you've already discovered a favourite South-East Asian author or are looking to venture into a new literary landscape, this is the perfect opportunity to read, discuss, and connect with fellow book lovers.

name: Jaya's Healing Circle - An Open Healing Circle
description:
We live in a world that rarely hits pause. Between the non-stop buzz of notifications, the pressure to "have it all together," and the daily juggle of work, family, and unpredictable life shifts, our nervous systems are constantly running on high alert.
While modern life keeps us more digitally connected than ever, it can also leave us feeling deeply isolated. We often swallow our anxieties, stress, grief, and even happiness, which we want to share, and there is no one around.
Human beings aren't built to carry heavy feelings alone. We need spaces where we can drop the armour, stop pretending, and just breathe. That is exactly why we created 
 
This Healing Circle - as an antidote to the exhausting pace of today's lifestyle and a reminder that your feelings deserve a soft place to land
You’re Invited to our Healing Circle - A safe space to breathe: 
If you’ve been looking for a place to put that weight down, even just for an hour or two, we would love for you to join us.
We are hosting a Free Healing Circle, a gentle, open space where you can share what’s truly on your mind and heart.
 
What to Expect:
This isn't a lecture or a rigid seminar. It is a space where you can express, emote and unburden yourself. You can talk about what’s troubling you, listen to others or simply sit in the quiet comfort of shared human connection. There is absolutely no pressure to speak if you just want to listen.
• A safe, non-judgmental atmosphere: A sacred space where every emotion is valid and welcome.
• True connection: An opportunity to realize that whatever you are feeling, you aren't carrying it alone.
• Guided support: The circle will be gently held by Jaya, an experienced 
therapist who brings years of warmth, wisdom, and deep empathy to her 
practice. Her presence ensures that everyone feels seen, heard, and 
emotionally safe. Come exactly as you are. Whether you are feeling sad, anxious, frustrated, just a little lost or elated, you are so welcome here.

 
What You’ll Take Away
You won’t leave with a to-do list or a set of rigid rules. Instead, by the end of our time together, you can expect to walk away with:
• A Lighter Heart: The simple, profound relief that comes from finally voicing what you've been carrying.
• A Sense of Belonging: The comforting realization that you are not alone in 
your struggles and that others truly understand.
• Emotional Grounding: Gentle coping insights from Jaya to help you navigate modern stress with a bit more ease and self-compassion.

name: Let Poetry Be - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This monthly gathering will see poetry coming to life, through reading, music, art and any other forms of artistic expression, at Atta Galatta. Listeners and performers are all welcome!
Date: 25th July
Time: 4pm
Venue: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
Event specifics & submission guidelines –
1. Theme - “Things in my drawer”(an item you are emotionally attached to!)
2. Works can be original or of other poets, performed live
3. All languages welcome
4. Participants can read to music (acoustic instrument/s), dramatise, use props & costumes
5. Each participant will enjoy 5 minutes of reading time
6. Kindly submit your poem and name to letpoetrybe@gmail.com on or before 23rd July midnight. WE WILL NOT ACCEPT ATTACHMENTS OR LINKS. SUBJECT OF YOUR MAIL MUST READ AS 'Entry for-July LPB meet-up'
7. You may also send entries that are not related to the theme
8. The event will begin at 4pm sharp and will be held at Atta Galatta. Participants to report not later than 3:45 pm to hear about performance order and meet the host
9. For any further clarifications, write to us at letpoetrybe@gmail.com
You are now welcome to flood our inbox!

name: This Isn't S*xy -
description:
Some ambitions are loud.
Others quietly follow you around for years.
The business.
The novel.
The healthier body.
The career change.
The city you want to move to.
The person you suspect you're capable of becoming.
And sometimes...
You don't even know what that thing is yet.
You just know your current life doesn't feel fully yours.
This Isn't S*xy isn't about motivation.
It's about sitting down for two uninterrupted hours to honestly ask yourself what you want—and building a system that makes it much harder to ignore once you know.
We'll map your priorities, challenge vague ideas, translate them into concrete actions, and leave with a personalized operating system for the months ahead.
No productivity gurus.
No life hacks.
No vision boards.
Just thoughtful conversations, paper, pencils, and a room full of people trying to build lives they're excited to wake up to.

name: Divya Leelamritham - Divine stories through Bharatanatyam
description:
Experience an enchanting evening of Bharatanatyam as timeless stories of Ganesha, Rama, Krishna, and Devi come alive through dance, music, and expression. A celebration of devotion, grace, and the joy of surrendering to the Divine, Divya Leelamritham invites audiences of all ages to immerse themselves in the beauty of India's rich cultural heritage.
Join us for an evening filled with devotion, storytelling, rhythm, and the timeless beauty of Bharatanatyam.

name: The Heart Algorithm - Book launch
description:
Join us for the launch of The Heart Algorithm: A Story of Runanubandha, the debut novel by Padma Iyer.
Set across Bangalore, California, and the Himalayas, the novel is a deeply moving exploration of love, resilience, medicine, and the invisible bonds that connect us. Blending emotional storytelling with medical realism and spirituality, The Heart Algorithm invites readers to reflect on the relationships that shape our lives.
Padma will be in conversation with Dr. Meryl Antony (MBBS, MD, St. John's Medical College Hospital), followed by a reading, audience Q&A, and an opportunity to meet the author.

name: Read A Kitaab - Book Club
description:
Tiny books. Lasting echoes. 
Some stories fit in your pocket but stay with you for years.
Join us as we celebrate the books under 250 pages that changed the way we think, feel, and see the world. Bring your favourite short read, discover new ones, and spend an evening with fellow readers.
📍Atta Galatta, Indira Nagar, Bengaluru
📅 Sunday, July 26th, 2026
⏰ 5 PM – 7 PM
Entry free. Registration mandatory.

name: Panchatantra for Adults - Storytelling and Interactive Session
description:
Join us at Atta Galatta for an evening that peels back the "children's stories" veneer to reveal the Panchatantra for what it truly is a manual on power, persuasion, and human nature.
A sophisticated treatise on human psychology, political thought, and strategy, the 90 min Interactive Storytelling Performance by Vikram Sridhar explores the hidden tales from the Panchatantra as a  Folk Narrative through humor, memory, and human consciousness
The Storytelling session will be a sit-down format like a traditional baithak, with a few chair seatings available.
 
Age: 18+
Timing: 6:00PM- 7:30PM
Tickets priced at Rs. 300/- per person

name: A Journey Through Books - An Evening with Namita Gokhale
description:
Join Namita Gokhale for a journey through books, an evening moderated by Shinie Antony. Come listen to her talk about the journey from her acclaimed debut Paro, to her latest work Voices in the Wind.
About Namita:
Namita Gokhale is a writer and festival director. She is the author of 27 works of fiction and no-fiction. Her acclaimed debut novel, Paro : Dreams of Passion was published in 1984, and has since been reissued as a Penguin Modern Classic. Recent fiction includes Never Never Land and Life on Mars. 
The Whispering Mountains and Voices in the Wind were published in 2025 and 2026. Her prolific oeuvre spans various genres , including novels, short stories, anthologies, Himalayan studies, folklore, mythology, books for young readers and a play. She is the recipient of several prizes and awards, including the Sahitya Akademi Award 2021 for her novel Things to Leave Behind. She is the co-founder and co-director of the famed Jaipur Literature Festival.

name: Kids Book Session with Mallika Ravikumar - Stories and games
description: Join us for an engaging session with children's author Mallika Ravikumar, whose football-themed novels The District Cup and The State League have captivated young readers. Come spend an evening filled with stories, sports, and interactive activities. The session will include storytelling, games, conversations about the books, and lots of opportunities to celebrate teamwork and imagination.

name: Not safe for work - Book launch
description:
Join us for the Bangalore launch of Not Safe for Work, the latest contemporary romance by Radhika Agrawal.
Set against the backdrop of corporate chaos, Not Safe for Work follows senior graphic designer Kavya Mehra and her infuriatingly charming intern Neil Desai as workplace rivalry, hidden secrets, and late-night brainstorming sessions blur the lines between professional and personal. Packed with witty banter, sizzling chemistry, and an irresistible enemies-to-lovers romance, it's a must-read for fans of modern rom-coms. 
The evening will feature Radhika in conversation with Aastha Sharma as they discuss writing romance, creating unforgettable characters, navigating the publishing journey, and everything that makes readers fall in love with a great love story.
About the Hosts:
Aastha Sharma is a community builder and tastemaker, founder of HappyStoryNook and The Bookstagram Collective. With 10 years across founders’ offices and marketing for India’s most loved consumer tech and lifestyle brands, she writes on Substack at the intersection of aesthetics, culture, and everyday life.
Radhika Agrawal is a lifestyle journalist and the author of the bestselling romance novel Red Flags and Rishtas. A sucker for all things filmy, she sees the world in meet-cutes and dream sequences. Originally from Delhi, she now lives in Thailand with her husband and their over-pampered dog, who hates competing with fictional characters for her attention.

name: Queer Quiet Hours - Meetup
description:
Queer Quiet Hours returns for August 2026!
Register here - http://bit.ly/adhdqueeple
 
A soft little corner for LGBTQIA+ folks to slow down, make art, read, journal, daydream, or simply exist in shared company.
 
There’s no pressure to interact. Come with your own project, or just arrive as you are. We’ll also have art materials around for anyone who wants to use them.
 
✨ This space is for LGBTQIA+ folks only. We haven’t opened it up to allies yet. Thank you for understanding.
 
🆓 Free to join, but spots are limited. Link in bio.
 
📅  9 AUGUST, 2026
⏰ Sundays, 2 - 5 PM
📍 @attagalatta, Indiranagar, Bangalore
📸 No photos taken without clear consent.

name: Curious Minds Trivia Night  - Trivia Night
description:
Forget everything you think a trivia night is. 
Curious Minds Trivia Night is an interactive, people-first experience that blends pop culture, teamwork, strategy, and friendly competition into one memorable evening. Before the event, participants fill out a short registration form sharing their favourite movies, TV shows, music, and interests. Parts of the trivia are then built around those responses, making every edition unique and tailored to the people in the room. Expect a mix of visual rounds, quote challenges, picture rounds, strategic betting rounds, and a few surprises along the way. Some rounds will test your memory, others your observation, and a few will reward teamwork, creativity, and quick thinking.
Whether you’re a seasoned trivia enthusiast or someone looking for a genuinely good evening with strangers — this event is designed to bring people together through laughter, conversation, and a little healthy rivalry.
Come with a team or walk in solo — we’ll find you one. All you need is curiosity and a willingness to play.
Come once. Come back forever

name: TechnoCats - Film Screening
description:
A search for purpose in the fiercely competitive world of the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT).
SYNOPSIS 
Seven young people from seven parts of the country (the Seven Samurai) dream of joining the IIT. Competing against one million applicants, they gain admission, but are soon disillusioned. Will they make their presence felt on the world stage? Or burn out along the way? The filmmaker, an IIT alumnus, follows the Seven Samurai over seven years—from their first semester to the third year of their careers. Interwoven with their journey are four of the filmmaker’s classmates from 35 years ago, including one who made an audiovisual on IIT student life. As he rediscovers old slides and struggles to sync them with the audio track, this forgotten past frames the film’s exploration of winning at all cost. 
DIRECTOR’S STATEMENT 
I enrolled for a B.Tech. in electrical engineering at IIT Delhi, but was dejected at the barrage of tests and emphasis on grades over all else. Where was the time to learn, ponder, speculate? 35 years later, I was surprised to meet students from very different backgrounds who felt similarly. I decided to revisit my past and connect with some of my classmates. Their stories are interwoven with  those of the seven students at the heart of this film, to create a conversation across generations.

name: The Next Curve Within - A Quarterly Flagship Experience by The Next Curve
description:
Some of life’s most meaningful transformations don’t begin around us—they begin within us.
The Next Curve Within is the flagship quarterly gathering by The Next Curve, bringing together inspiring voices from leadership, health, longevity, mindfulness and sustainability to help us prepare for the most important years of our lives.
This is not another seminar. It is an immersive afternoon of ideas, lived experiences and authentic conversations designed for those who believe that growing older should mean growing stronger, healthier, wiser and more purposeful.
This edition explores five dimensions of transformation:

Finding clarity through meditation and mindful living.
Building a regenerative future through water and conscious action.
Reinventing life after 75 with purpose and vitality.
Preventing disease by embracing a healthier way of life.
Understanding how healthy, resilient leaders build high-performing organisations through physical, mental and emotional transformation.

Whether you are an entrepreneur, corporate leader, professional, educator, healthcare practitioner, student or someone committed to lifelong growth, Within offers insights that go beyond knowledge and inspire meaningful action.
Event Details
Date: Saturday, 19th September 2026
Time: 3:00 PM – 5:00 PM
Venue: Atta Galatta, Bengaluru
Access Pass: ₹300 per person
Featured Speakers

Cherian Kuruvila – The Quiet Revolution
Vivek Srinivasan – Every Drop Matters
Hari Bhaskaran – Life Begins at 75
Dr. Dharini – Prevention Before Prescription
Keynote: Salma Moosa – Drive to Thrive: Building Fit & Healthy Leaders to Build Fast-Growing Organisations

The evening concludes with Conversations That Matter—a guided networking experience where meaningful connections are built through thoughtful dialogue rather than business card exchanges.
The Next Curve Within is for those ready to reinvent ageing, regenerate the planet and redesign themselves—one conversation at a time.
